text: is the VOA Special English Health Report.
zh: 这里是美国之音特别英语健康报道。

text: Cardiovascular disease is the world's leading cause of
zh: 心血管疾病是全球头号致死原因，

text: death. It includes heart attack, stroke, and high blood pressure.
zh: 它包括心脏病发作、中风和高血压。

text: Over the years, researchers have identified several substances in the blood
zh: 多年来，研究人员在血液中发现了多种物质，

text: that can serve as what they call cardiac biomarkers.
zh: 这些物质可以作为所谓的心脏生物标志物。

text: These are used to measure the presence and development of cardiovascular disease.
zh: 它们被用来衡量心血管疾病的存在与发展情况。

text: Researchers have increasingly tried to use these biomarkers
zh: 研究人员越来越多地尝试利用这些生物标志物，

text: to identify people who are at high risk of developing heart disease,
zh: 来识别患心脏病高风险的人群，

text: but a new study has found that they offer little help in this way.
zh: 但一项新研究发现，它们在这方面帮助不大。

text: A a team from Massachusetts General Hospital
zh: 麻省总医院

text: and Sweden's Lund University studied how effective the biomarkers are
zh: 和瑞典隆德大学的一个团队研究了这些生物标志物

text: as predictors. Thomas Wang at the Mass General Heart Center was the senior author of the study.
zh: 作为预测指标的有效性。麻省总医院心脏中心的托马斯·王是该研究的资深作者。

text: Dr. Wang said that even after measuring those additional biomarkers,
zh: 王博士说，即便测量了这些额外的生物标志物，

text: they were not able to fully understand who was more likely to develop heart disease.
zh: 他们仍无法完全判断谁更可能患上心脏病。

text: Dr. Wang says they did identify some combinations of biomarkers
zh: 王博士说，他们确实发现了一些生物标志物组合，

text: that improved predictions of heart attacks and strokes.
zh: 能够改善对心脏病发作和中风的预测。

text: He said it is possible that in certain patients,
zh: 他说，对于某些患者来说，

text: measuring these biomarkers would be helpful.
zh: 测量这些生物标志物可能是有帮助的。

text: But for the majority of patients, having the information of the biomarker
zh: 但对大多数患者而言，掌握这些生物标志物的信息

text: probably would not make a difference.
zh: 可能并不会带来什么不同。

text: Dr. Wang hopes future research will discover biomarkers that are better able to predict the risk of cardiovascular disease.
zh: 王博士希望未来的研究能发现更能预测心血管疾病风险的生物标志物。

text: But for now, he says, doctors should depend on traditional risk factors.
zh: 但他说，就目前而言，医生应依靠传统的风险因素。

text: These include a history of high blood pressure, high cholesterol,
zh: 这些因素包括高血压病史、高胆固醇、

text: tobacco use, diabetes, obesity, physical inactivity, or poor nutrition.
zh: 吸烟、糖尿病、肥胖、缺乏运动或营养不良。

text: A separate study found no support for a theory that a biomarker called
zh: 另一项研究未能支持这样一种理论，即一种名为

text: C -reactive protein causes heart disease.
zh: C反应蛋白的生物标志物会导致心脏病。

text: Earlier research suggested that the more of the protein in people's blood,
zh: 早期研究表明，人体血液中这种蛋白质含量越高，

text: the more likely they are to develop heart disease.
zh: 患心脏病的可能性就越大。

text: The new study confirmed a link, but did not find evidence that the C -reactive protein
zh: 新研究证实了两者之间存在关联，但没有发现证据表明C反应蛋白

text: causes the disease. Both studies appeared in the Journal of the American Medical Association.
zh: 会导致该疾病。这两项研究均发表在《美国医学会杂志》上。

text: The World Health Organization estimates that cardiovascular disease
zh: 世界卫生组织估计，

text: killed 17 .5 million people in 2005.
zh: 2005年心血管疾病导致1750万人死亡。

text: That was 30 % of all deaths.
zh: 这占所有死亡人数的30%。

text: Eight out of ten deaths were in low - and middle -income countries.
zh: 十分之八的死亡发生在中低收入国家。

text: At current growth rates, the WHO expects the number to reach 20 million by 2015.
zh: 按照目前的增长速度，世卫组织预计到2015年这一数字将达到2000万。

text: And that's the VOA Special English Health Report.
zh: 以上就是美国之音特别英语健康报道。
